name: tailwind-v4-shadcn
description:
Set up Tailwind v4 with shadcn/ui using @theme inline pattern and CSS variable architecture. Four-step pattern: CSS variables, Tailwind mapping, base styles, automatic dark mode. Prevents 8 documented errors.

Use when initializing React projects with Tailwind v4, or fixing colors not working, tw-animate-css errors, @theme inline dark mode conflicts, @apply breaking, v3 migration issues.
capabilities: Initialize Tailwind v4 with @theme inline pattern using CSS variables
Configure shadcn/ui components with proper CSS variable architecture
Implement automatic dark mode detection using CSS prefers-color-scheme
Guide through the four-step setup pattern: CSS variables, Tailwind mapping, base styles, dark mode
Fix common errors: tw-animate-css issues, @theme conflicts, @apply breaking, v3 migration
author: jezweb
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation files (markdown, templates) with no executable code. Scanners misinterpreted CSS @theme declarations as cryptographic algorithms and markdown code formatting as shell execution. This is a legitimate Claude Code development skill.
valueStatement: Claude often makes errors when setting up Tailwind CSS v4 with shadcn/ui. This skill guides Claude through the correct four-step pattern for CSS variables, Tailwind mapping, base styles, and automatic dark mode, preventing 8 documented errors.
userTitle: Set up Tailwind v4 with shadcn/ui
limitations: Does not install dependencies automatically - guides Claude on correct installation order
Does not modify existing project configuration files - only creates new ones
Does not handle complex custom Tailwind configurations beyond the standard v4 pattern
Requires Claude to execute terminal commands to apply the guidance

useCases.title: Initialize a new React project with Tailwind v4
useCases.description: Set up a fresh React project with Tailwind CSS v4 using the modern @theme inline pattern and CSS variable architecture for shadcn/ui components.

useCases.title: Migrate from Tailwind v3 to v4
useCases.description: Follow the correct migration pattern to upgrade existing Tailwind v3 projects to v4 while maintaining shadcn/ui compatibility.

useCases.title: Fix broken Tailwind v4 setup
useCases.description: Diagnose and fix issues with colors not working, animation errors, or dark mode conflicts in existing Tailwind v4 configurations.

promptTemplates.title: Troubleshooting setup
promptTemplates.prompt: My Tailwind v4 colors are not working and I'm seeing tw-animate-css errors. Use the tailwind-v4-shadcn skill to fix my configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Dark mode configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up automatic dark mode with Tailwind v4 and shadcn/ui. Use the tailwind-v4-shadcn skill to configure CSS variables for light and dark themes.

bestPractices: Define CSS variables before configuring @theme mapping
Use the @theme directive to map variables to Tailwind utilities
Configure dark mode using CSS prefers-color-scheme, not JavaScript
Install dependencies in the correct order: CSS variables first, then Tailwind utilities
antiPatterns: Do not mix Tailwind v3 config files with v4 @theme pattern
Do not use inline styles for colors when CSS variables are available
Do not skip the four-step setup pattern - each step builds on the previous
Do not forget to configure base styles and CSS reset

faq.question: What is the difference between Tailwind v3 and v4 config?
faq.answer: Tailwind v4 uses the new @theme directive for inline CSS configuration instead of the traditional tailwind.config.js file. This allows defining CSS variables and mapping them directly in CSS without JavaScript configuration.

faq.question: How does dark mode work with Tailwind v4?
faq.answer: Dark mode uses CSS prefers-color-scheme to automatically detect system preferences. The theme-provider component toggles a class on the HTML element, and CSS variables switch between light and dark values.

faq.question: Can I use this with existing shadcn/ui projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill is designed specifically for shadcn/ui. It follows the shadcn/ui pattern of using CSS variables for all colors, making the setup compatible with their component library.

faq.question: What errors does this skill prevent?
faq.answer: The skill prevents 8 documented errors including tw-animate-css loading failures, @theme inline conflicts, @apply breaking on custom properties, and v3 migration issues.

faq.question: Does this skill install packages automatically?
faq.answer: No, the skill provides guidance on the correct commands and order but requires Claude to execute terminal commands to install packages and create files.

faq.question: Can I use this with frameworks other than React?
faq.answer: The core Tailwind v4 setup works with any framework. The shadcn/ui-specific parts require React, but the CSS variable and @theme patterns are framework-agnostic.
